name: opencastle
script_text:
# OpenCastle: Multi-Agent AI Coding Orchestration

OpenCastle transforms single AI coding assistants into coordinated multi-agent teams. It works across GitHub Copilot, Cursor, Claude Code, OpenCode, Windsurf, Codex, and Antigravity through unified orchestration setup.

The framework decomposes development tasks into specialized agents that work in parallel and verify each other's output. Deploy agents interactively through IDE chat or automate overnight via the Convoy Engine.

With nine active contributors shipping improvements, the project handles IDE-specific configurations automatically—generating tailored agent rules, skills, and MCP servers for your stack in a single command.

Use this for scaling AI-assisted development beyond single-assistant limitations.

name: adk-java
script_text:
# ADK-Java: Code-First AI Agent Development

ADK-Java is an open-source toolkit for building and deploying sophisticated AI agents entirely in code. Developers define agent behavior, tool integration, and orchestration directly in Java—enabling precise control, easier testing, and seamless versioning across environments.

The toolkit features pre-built tools, custom function support, and OpenAPI integration for diverse agent capabilities. Multi-agent systems can be composed into scalable hierarchies for complex applications. With 28 active contributors and ongoing momentum, the project continues gaining traction in the community.

ADK-Java is designed for teams building advanced AI agents tightly integrated with Google Cloud services.

name: tidesdb
script_text:
# TidesDB: High-Performance Embedded Storage

TidesDB is a C-based key-value storage engine built on log-structured merge-trees, designed for flash and RAM optimization. It delivers ACID transactions with five isolation levels, including serializable snapshot isolation, plus multi-column family support with independent configuration.

The engine features adaptive compaction modes that automatically scale capacities, bidirectional iterators with snapshot isolation, and optional tiered object storage for infinite scaling. Key-value separation reduces write amplification, while crash recovery reconstructs state from write-ahead logs.

With active development and steady momentum from contributors, TidesDB serves as an embeddable foundation for building custom databases and high-performance key-value stores without full database overhead.

name: context
script_text:
# Context: AI-Ready Codebase Analysis

Context is a CLI tool that analyzes your codebase and generates optimized context packs for AI coding assistants like Claude and Cursor. Instead of manually selecting files, Context automatically discovers relevant code by scanning import graphs, detecting frameworks, and analyzing task descriptions.

The tool indexes your project once, then creates focused context packages containing only the files needed for specific tasks. Each pack includes dependency graphs, code excerpts, and a structured prompt ready for AI agents. It supports TypeScript, JavaScript, PHP, Python, and detects frameworks like Laravel, NestJS, and React automatically.

Use Context to give AI assistants precise context, reducing hallucinations and improving code generation accuracy.

name: anakin
script_text:
# AnakinScraper: Open-Source Web Scraping API

AnakinScraper turns any website into clean markdown or structured JSON with a single command. Built in Go, it combines an anti-detect browser, intelligent proxy selection, and handler chain fallbacks—HTTP fetch, browser rendering, or external APIs—to maximize success rates while minimizing API costs.

Self-host locally with zero cloud dependencies. The modular architecture lets you define per-domain scraping strategies, detect failures, and retry automatically. With four active contributors driving recent momentum, the project powers RAG pipelines and AI data extraction at scale.

Deploy with `make up`. Perfect for feeding LLMs clean, structured data.

name: nanobot
script_text:
# nanobot: Ultra-Lightweight Personal AI Agent

nanobot is an open-source AI agent designed for minimal overhead and maximum readability. The core loop stays simple while supporting chat channels, memory management, and the Model Context Protocol for practical deployment.

Built on Python 3.11 or higher with MIT licensing, nanobot connects to multiple platforms—Discord, Slack, Teams, and Feishu. Recent momentum shows 646 active contributors engaging across 131 pull requests and 52 issues in the last month, driving features like thread awareness, web search integration, and provider flexibility.

The architecture supports both local and cloud deployment paths, letting developers transition from personal setup to production-grade long-running agents. Perfect for building autonomous assistants that stay lean and maintainable.

name: OpenViking
script_text:
# OpenViking: Context Database for AI Agents

OpenViking is an open-source context database built specifically for AI agents. It unifies memory, resources, and skills management through a file system paradigm, enabling hierarchical context delivery and self-evolution.

Traditional AI agent development fragments context across code, vector databases, and scattered skill repositories. OpenViking solves this with a single, organized context layer that supports long-running tasks without information loss and provides observable retrieval chains for debugging.

The project maintains strong momentum with 513 active contributors processing 102 pull requests and 58 issues in the last month. Designed for agents like OpenClaw, it's essential infrastructure for production-grade agent systems requiring structured, scalable context management.

name: anthropic-tools
script_text:
# anthropic-tools Video Script

Anthropic-tools provides a lightweight framework for integrating tool use and function calling with Claude models. The SDK introduces two core classes—BaseTool and ToolUser—that simplify defining custom tools and managing their execution. Developers inherit BaseTool, implement a use_tool method, and pass parameters to Claude for structured execution. The package includes pre-built tools for common operations like web search and SQL queries, all built on the same extensible primitives. Note: this repository is deprecated. For production implementations, use Anthropic's official API documentation. Best suited for developers prototyping function-calling architectures with Claude.

name: open-agents
script_text:
**Open Agents: Building Cloud-Native Coding Agents**

Open Agents is an open-source template for building autonomous coding agents on Vercel. The system separates the agent runtime from sandbox execution—a critical architectural choice allowing independent scaling and evolution of each layer.

The platform includes chat-driven coding capabilities, durable multi-step execution via Workflow SDK, isolated sandbox environments, and GitHub integration for automated commits and pull requests. Recent activity shows strong momentum with 443 contributors engaging across 13 pull requests in the last month.

Developers fork and adapt the template rather than treating it as a black box. Built for teams needing background agents that operate autonomously without tying up local resources.

name: 1code
script_text:
# 1Code: Orchestration Layer for Coding Agents

1Code is an open-source client that orchestrates multiple coding agents—Claude Code, Codex, and others—locally or in the cloud. It isolates each agent session in a Git worktree, preventing accidental commits to main while maintaining full branch safety. The interface mirrors Cursor with real-time diff previews, integrated Git operations, and live browser testing. Unique features include background execution for cloud sandboxes, MCP plugin support, chat forking, and extended thinking enabled by default. The built-in terminal, visual staging, and model switcher keep developers in one workspace. Use 1Code to run coding agents safely with complete visibility over changes before they land.

name: OpenViking
script_text:
# OpenViking: Context Database for AI Agents

OpenViking is an open-source context database built specifically for AI agents. It solves a critical problem: fragmented memory, resources, and skills scattered across code and vector databases.

Instead of traditional flat storage, OpenViking uses a file system paradigm to organize context hierarchically. This approach enables developers to manage agent memory the same way they manage local files—structured, observable, and scalable.

The system handles context that grows from continuous agent execution without information loss. It provides transparent retrieval chains for debugging and supports agent-specific task memory, not just user interaction logs.

Perfect for developers building long-running AI agents that need reliable context management.

name: openai-agents-python
script_text:
# OpenAI Agents Python SDK Video Script

The OpenAI Agents SDK is a lightweight framework for building multi-agent systems in Python. It supports 100+ LLMs and includes built-in tools for agent orchestration, guardrails, human-in-the-loop controls, and tracing. 

Key features include sandbox agents for long-running tasks, agent handoffs for task delegation, session management for conversation history, and real-time voice capabilities with GPT-4 integration.

With 131 active contributors recently engaging across 38 pull requests and 17 issues, the project demonstrates strong development momentum. Installation requires Python 3.10+ via pip or uv.

Use this SDK to build scalable multi-agent systems that coordinate complex workflows across distributed LLMs.

name: mcp
script_text:
# mcp - Code Graph Analysis for AI Agents

This MCP server delivers instant codebase understanding through pre-computed code graphs. Developers integrate it with AI agents to analyze call graphs, dependency structures, detect dead code, and calculate blast radius—all with sub-second response times.

The technical advantage: pre-computed graph analysis eliminates expensive token-heavy code traversals. Agents can perform complex cross-subsystem analysis without repeated context window consumption, reducing token costs by up to 40 percent.

Installation is straightforward via npm or a single setup script. Configuration requires only an API key to start. This tool targets teams optimizing AI agent efficiency on large codebases.

name: MiroFish
script_text:
# MiroFish: Engineering Script

MiroFish is a swarm intelligence engine that simulates multi-agent systems to predict outcomes across domains. Developers feed seed data—news reports, policy documents, financial signals—and natural language queries describing the prediction target. The system automatically constructs a high-fidelity digital world where thousands of autonomous agents with independent personalities, memory, and behavioral logic interact and evolve. Users then observe emergent patterns and inject variables in real-time to test hypothetical scenarios. The technical foundation leverages multi-agent modeling to capture collective behavior from individual interactions, enabling granular forecasting without traditional statistical constraints. Use cases span policy simulation, narrative prediction, and financial scenario testing.

name: claude-plugins
script_text:
# Claude-Plugins: Engineering-Focused Script

Claude-Plugins is a marketplace for Claude Code featuring specialized productivity extensions. The humanlayer plugin provides six core slash commands and six research agents for codebase analysis, planning, and implementation workflows. The jj plugin adds Jujutsu version control support with atomic commits and stack-aware PR creation.

Installation is straightforward: add the marketplace via GitHub, then install individual plugins. The project maintains active development momentum with ongoing contributor engagement on pull requests.

These plugins streamline the complete development cycle from research through validation, making them essential for teams managing complex codebases and multi-step development workflows.

name: AI-Agent-Framework
script_text:
# AI-Agent-Framework Video Script

Agency is a Python library implementing the Actor model for building agent-integrated systems. It provides a minimal foundation to connect AI agents with traditional software through a straightforward API: define agents as classes, expose actions as methods, and control access via policies.

The framework supports multiprocessing, multithreading, and AMQP for networked deployments. Built-in callbacks enable lifecycle management and observability. The library includes working examples with OpenAI, HuggingFace transformers, and OS-level access.

Use Agency when you need to build custom agent-based applications that scale across distributed systems.

name: GlyphLang
script_text:
# GlyphLang: AI-Optimized Programming Language

GlyphLang is a backend language designed specifically for AI code generation. It cuts boilerplate by up to 75 percent compared to Python frameworks, reducing LLM token costs by 23 percent while maintaining production-grade type safety.

The language compiles to a single static binary with built-in HTTP routing, async support, and database access. No external servers or configuration files needed. Type definitions and routes are the program itself.

With active development momentum—6 pull requests and 3 issue discussions in the last month—the project is moving fast. Developers building AI-powered APIs or using LLMs for backend generation will find the minimal syntax and structured validation directly reduce generation errors and maintenance overhead.
